sourceMovie.title: Hamilton
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hamilton (2025) stuck because it commits to its history premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Thomas Kail's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Les Misérables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hamilton clicked for you, Les Misérables (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hamilton?
faq.answer: Try Les Misérables, October Sky, The Young Victoria—each shares DNA with Hamilton while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hamilton?
faq.answer: Hamilton (2025) is best described as History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hamilton worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
